page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg6.16xlarge is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the ecs.hfg6 family, powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 64 physical Intel Xeon 8269CY cores on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, this instance ensures dedicated, non-hyperthreaded compute performance. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. The dedicated CPU allocation with a 1:1 physical-core-to-thread ratio ensures consistent compute performance without resource contention from hyperthreading, offering high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require predictable CPU execution.

Given its balanced compute-to-memory ratio and high-frequency dedicated Intel Xeon cores, the ecs.hfg6.16xlarge is suited for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance and moderate memory capacity. Typical use cases include web serving, application servers, database hosting, and general-purpose enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg6.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance from the ecs.hfg6 family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 64 dedicated vCPUs across 64 physical Intel Xeon 8269CY cores running at 3.1 GHz, with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The instance features 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. This hardware profile makes the instance highly suitable for workloads requiring predictable, non-hyperthreaded compute performance, such as application servers, database hosting, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
